evals.prompt: Enable Opus/Fable thinking summaries in my VS Code Claude Code extension by applying the display patch.
assertions: The --thinking-display chokepoint is rewritten so an omitted display defaults to "summarized"
A pristine .js.orig backup is created for the modified display bundle
Re-running the display patch is idempotent (reports ALREADY PATCHED, no further change)

evals.prompt: Also make thinking blocks default to expanded each session (the optional additional patch).
assertions: The expand-state useState(!1) is flipped to useState(!0) in the areThinkingBlocksExpanded webview bundle
A pristine .js.orig backup is created for the modified webview bundle
Re-running the expand patch is idempotent (reports ALREADY PATCHED, no further change)

evals.prompt: Undo both patches and restore my extension to its original state.
assertions: restore both reverts the display bundle byte-for-byte to pristine
restore both reverts the expand/webview bundle byte-for-byte to pristine
all .js.orig backups are consumed/removed after restore both
